name: Security scripts · Incident response toolkit (Windows + Linux)
description: Modular PowerShell and Bash scripts for endpoint containment, investigation, eradication, and verification—phased IR as code with logging, scoping, and chain-of-custody awareness.
longDescription: This toolkit packages incident-response playbooks as code: Windows paths cover containment, investigation, eradication, and recovery with parameterised entrypoints (hostname, user, timeframe, scope). Linux scripts support server hunting across multiple targets. The toolkit emphasises repeatable execution, structured logging, and separation of duties between phases so teams can coordinate during live incidents without collapsing containment and evidence collection into a single opaque step. Outputs are treated as sensitive intelligence products.
features: Windows IR orchestration scripts with scoped parameters—reduce blast radius of automation
Linux hunt scripts for multi-host triage
Documented directory layout for each IR phase (containment vs forensics vs recovery)
Quick-start snippets for administrators with prerequisites called out explicitly
Structured logging hooks so actions are attributable and reviewable after the fact
highlights: Bridges Windows endpoint and Linux server response workflows
Emphasises phased IR over one-off manual commands
challenges: Incident scripts are powerful; misuse can destroy evidence or cause downtime.
solutions: Document prerequisites, scope flags, and dry-run guidance; require administrative intent before running destructive phases.
security: Run only on systems you own or are authorised to investigate
Protect script outputs—they may contain sensitive forensic data and credentials
Review commands before execution in regulated environments; prefer dry-runs where supported
Maintain separation between destructive remediation steps and evidence preservation where legal process requires
Store tooling and logs with access controls equivalent to tier-0 operational assets
